portfolio: Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sport & Arts
executive_summary: The National Archives is a medium-sized (~390 staff), non-corporate Commonwealth entity undertaking a strategy-driven transformation (Strategy 2025-2030: Evolving National Archives) centred on becoming a 'digital-first' archive. Its dominant pressures are the explosion of born-digital records, an ageing at-risk physical/AV collection requiring urgent digitisation, legacy manual workflows, cyber threat, and a tight fiscal position where the plan openly states expenses 'appear likely to exceed available appropriations'. The agency is also pursuing modernisation of the Archives Act 1983 and leads whole-of-government information management through the 'Building trust in the public record' policy. For BD purposes this is principally a Digital & ICT Delivery, Data/Information and Financial-Sustainability story, sized to a smaller cultural/data agency rather than large-scale program delivery. Note the plan is dated 2025-26 to 2028-29 despite the source URL referencing the 2024-25-2027-28 plan.

strategic_priorities.title: Build a digital-first, end-to-end archive
strategic_priorities.description: Transition all aspects of work to digital, accelerate development of a digital-first archive, streamline ingestion of born-digital records, integrate legacy systems and automate manual workflows.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: National Archives is transitioning to a digital-first approach in all aspects of our work... integrating our existing systems and creating digital workflows that increase effectiveness and efficiency.

strategic_priorities.title: Preserve and digitise at-risk collections
strategic_priorities.description: Prioritise digitisation and preservation of records at risk of loss or deterioration (chemically unstable, obsolete physical and digital formats), with a focus on First Nations material; meet targets of 20%+ of at-risk physical items digitised and 95%+ of digital items in low-risk stable formats.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: identify and prioritise the digitisation of records that are at risk of loss or damage, in particular records of importance to First Nations peoples

strategic_priorities.title: Lead whole-of-government information management
strategic_priorities.description: Drive uptake of the 'Building trust in the public record' policy and lift information management maturity across agencies via the annual Check-up survey and targeted agency engagement; redevelop Agency Engagement and Digital Archiving Service models.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: strengthening our leadership in Australian Government information management... support the effective implementation of the Building trust in the public record policy

strategic_priorities.title: Modernise the Archives Act 1983
strategic_priorities.description: Pursue amendments to the Archives Act, a Revive deliverable, to reflect the digital age and resolve ambiguities; the Act currently lacks any enforcement power over agency compliance.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: pursue amendments to the Archives Act 1983... that reflect the complexities of the digital age

strategic_priorities.title: Improve access, search and discovery
strategic_priorities.description: Deliver improvements in public search, external identity management, payment processing and content delivery; uplift metadata management and reparative/culturally appropriate description, including for First Nations collections.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: deliver improvements in our public search, external identity management, payment processing and content delivery

strategic_priorities.title: Financial sustainability
strategic_priorities.description: Manage a structural gap where ongoing expenses are likely to exceed appropriations; pursue efficiencies, consolidate repositories, scale storage and review the state/territory service-delivery model.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: expenses appear likely to exceed available appropriations. We continue to look for efficiencies and reduce our costs.

reform_and_change_drivers: Strategy 2025-2030: Evolving National Archives, driving an organisation-wide transformation
'Building trust in the public record' whole-of-government information management policy
National Cultural Policy (Revive) commitments, including 'First Nations first' and Archives Act reform
Archives Act 1983 modernisation agenda (no current enforcement power over agency compliance)
Shift to digital-by-default across government producing ever-growing volumes of born-digital records
Functional and Efficiency Review (Tune Review) and Government response implementation
Tight fiscal environment with expenses likely to exceed appropriations
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework targets to reduce outsourcing of Information and Knowledge Management work
Redevelopment of Agency Engagement and Digital Archiving Service models (reflected in revised PBS targets)
challenges_and_risks: Digital archiving systems and practice not keeping pace with rapid technological change
Archival material lost or destroyed through obsolescence, resource limitations, neglect or hostile action
Agencies not meeting Archives Act / information management obligations (a shared risk, with no enforcement lever)
Delayed public access to the collection due to manual processes and limited resources
Failure to connect with audiences, particularly First Nations and diverse communities
Agency capability (people, processes, technology) not meeting operational requirements, especially digital-first workforce
Cyber security threat landscape evolving as threat-actor capability grows
Financial sustainability: structural gap between growing demand/costs and appropriations
Expanding storage needs (physical and digital) for a continually growing collection

news_and_controversies.summary: Commentary framing the National Archives as being in 'crisis', focused on funding pressure and deteriorating at-risk records and magnetic-media digitisation.
news_and_controversies.implication: A live preservation/digitisation backlog signals demand for digitisation, data-migration and program-delivery support, within a scrutinised, constrained budget.
news_and_controversies.source: The Conversation, 'Our history up in flames? Why the crisis at the National Archives must be urgently addressed' — https://theconversation.com/our-history-up-in-flames-why-the-crisis-at-the-national-archives-must-be-urgently-addressed-159804

news_and_controversies.summary: Arts-sector advocacy calling for the National Archives to be protected/saved amid continuing concern over capacity and resourcing.
news_and_controversies.implication: Ongoing external pressure on capacity suggests appetite for capability uplift; engagements should be framed around public-value outcomes given reputational sensitivity.
news_and_controversies.source: ArtsHub, 'Why we need to save the National Archives of Australia' (22 Nov 2023) — https://www.artshub.com.au/news/opinions-analysis/why-we-need-to-save-the-national-archives-of-australia-262437-2370815/

news_and_controversies.summary: Trade-press reporting (tied to annual reporting) that the National Archives had 'struggled to fulfil its mandate'.
news_and_controversies.implication: Documented performance/mandate gaps underpin a value proposition around operating-model, performance and delivery improvement.
news_and_controversies.source: The Mandarin, 'National Archives struggled to fulfil its mandate' (18 Oct 2024) — https://www.themandarin.com.au/151501-national-archives-struggled-to-fulfil-its-mandate/

news_and_controversies.summary: The NAA was subject to a Functional and Efficiency Review (the Tune Review), with a published Australian Government response.
news_and_controversies.implication: A formal review plus government response typically generates an implementation/reform program — a natural entry point for advisory, PMO and change-delivery work.
news_and_controversies.source: National Archives of Australia, 'Tune Review of the National Archives of Australia' — https://www.naa.gov.au/about-us/tune-review ; Attorney-General's Department response — https://www.ag.gov.au/rights-and-protections/publications/australian-government-response-tune-review

news_and_controversies.summary: The NAA maintains a dedicated 'Defend the Past, Protect the Future' program page addressing preservation/digitisation of at-risk records.
news_and_controversies.implication: A named, funded program is a concrete vehicle through which digitisation/preservation services may be procured.

opportunity_areas.area: Digital-first archive build and systems integration
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Legacy processes are largely manual; the agency wants to integrate existing systems, create automated digital workflows, and streamline born-digital ingestion at scale.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A discovery-to-delivery engagement: business/process analysis of ingestion and access workflows, service design, and systems integration/automation to build the digital-first ingestion and workflow pipeline. The firm's ability to build (not just advise) is the differentiator.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly names integrating systems, automating manual processes and accelerating a digital-first archive as core to Strategy 2025-2030 and a key strategic risk. The 'Defend the Past, Protect the Future' program and a digital-first mandate provide a concrete vehicle.

opportunity_areas.area: At-risk records digitisation and digital preservation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Large backlog of at-risk physical, film and magnetic-media records requiring digitisation, plus migration of digital items into low-risk stable formats and obsolescence management.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Scaled digitisation and format-migration delivery: automated workflows and integration to lift at-risk physical digitisation (target >=20%) and move digital items into stable formats (target >=95%), with obsolescence-management tooling.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Performance measures S2.1-S2.3 set explicit, recurring digitisation and preservation targets; external commentary flags a digitisation/preservation 'crisis'. This is a measurable, funded need.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Discoverability depends on rich metadata and effective public search; the agency plans to improve public search, external identity management, payment processing and content delivery, and to apply emerging tech to metadata at scale.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An information/metadata strategy and management engagement: design of a scalable metadata model, automated metadata capture/enrichment, and search-and-discovery improvement (with Digital & ICT Delivery as secondary for build).
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan dedicates a section to metadata management and search/discovery, and removed the prior item-description target while it considers 'new approaches and technology' — an open signal for data/information capability help.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Expenses are likely to exceed appropriations over the long term; the agency is seeking efficiencies, repository consolidation and storage scaling under fiscal pressure.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability review and activity-based costing of core functions (storage, digitisation, access examination, state/territory offices) to identify efficiencies and inform funding/repository-consolidation decisions.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly states expenses appear likely to exceed appropriations and names cost reduction and repository consolidation as priorities; the Tune Review context reinforces an efficiency focus.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: The agency commits to reviewing the model for state/territory service and program delivery and redeveloping Agency Engagement and Digital Archiving Service models.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A target operating model / functional review of the state-and-territory service-delivery footprint and the Agency Engagement and Digital Archiving Service models, with change management support (Organisation, Workforce & People secondary).
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan states it will review the model for service and program delivery and is actively redeveloping two service models (cited as the reason PBS targets were revised down) — a clear, named operating-model need.

opportunity_areas.area: Transformation delivery and project management capability
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Strategy 2025-2030 transformation requires robust project management; the plan admits a need to uplift project management capability for timely, resource-optimised delivery.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: PMO setup and delivery assurance for the Strategy 2025-2030 portfolio, plus a project-management capability uplift, with embedded delivery support where APS Strategic Commissioning constraints allow.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names project management as a capability to build and lists agency capability as a strategic risk; a strategy-wide transformation needs delivery structure. Note Strategic Commissioning Framework may favour capability transfer over long embedded resourcing.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Evolving cyber threat landscape; agency commits to proactively uplift controls aligned to ACSC guidance, the ISM and PSPF.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A security assessment and assurance of digital archiving systems and the collection against ISM/PSPF controls, with a prioritised uplift roadmap.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan flags cyber as an operating-context challenge and embeds 'cyber secure technology' in its preservation risk control; a digital-first archive raises the stakes for securing the collection.

opportunity_areas.area: Tune Review / Archives Act reform implementation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Implementing the Functional and Efficiency Review response and preparing for Archives Act amendments will require policy, governance and implementation support.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Implementation and governance support for the Tune Review response and Archives Act modernisation readiness — governance framework, implementation roadmap and policy advisory.
opportunity_areas.rationale: A published government response to the Tune Review and a stated Revive commitment to amend the Archives Act create a multi-year reform agenda; the Act's lack of enforcement power is a named structural risk.

opportunity_areas.area: Whole-of-government information management maturity and evaluation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: The agency drives information management maturity across agencies via the Check-up survey and 'Building trust in the public record' policy, and analyses results to build capability.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Analytics and reporting uplift for the annual Check-up survey — improved maturity-index analytics, benchmarking dashboards and insight reporting to target agency engagement (Strategy, Policy & Governance secondary for evaluation).
opportunity_areas.rationale: Performance measures E1.1/E1.2 rely on Check-up survey data and maturity indices; the agency commits to analysing and using results to build capability across the Commonwealth.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the executive owner of Strategy 2025-2030 / Digital Archiving Service (likely the Assistant Director-General for digital/collection and the Director-General's office) with a tailored capability statement on digital-first archive delivery, integration and at-risk digitisation.
recommended_actions.rationale: Digital-first transformation and at-risk digitisation are the clearest, target-backed, funded needs in the plan and align with the firm's build-and-implement differentiator.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short financial-sustainability / costing thought piece for the CFO / Chief Operating Officer that links the 'expenses likely to exceed appropriations' admission to an activity-based costing and efficiency approach.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan openly concedes a structural funding gap and prioritises efficiencies and repository consolidation; this is a credible, lower-political-sensitivity entry point.

recommended_actions.action: Request a meeting on the state/territory service-delivery model review and Agency Engagement / Digital Archiving Service model redevelopment, leading with a target-operating-model offering.
recommended_actions.rationale: These reviews are explicitly named and were the stated reason PBS targets were revised down — indicating active, near-term work.

recommended_actions.action: Re-run ANAO and Parliamentary research (site:anao.gov.au and site:aph.gov.au for 'National Archives of Australia', Senate Estimates, Environment and Communications committee) before any client-facing pitch.
recommended_actions.rationale: External research was cut short; ANAO and Parliamentary signals are likely (recordkeeping audits, Estimates on funding/preservation) and would materially strengthen the assurance and financial value propositions.

recommended_actions.action: Develop a cyber and metadata/search position paper for the digital/ICT leadership, mindful of APS Strategic Commissioning Framework limits on outsourcing core Information and Knowledge Management work.
recommended_actions.rationale: Both are real needs but the Commissioning Framework explicitly targets reduced outsourcing of IKM work, so offerings should emphasise capability transfer and specialised build rather than ongoing managed services.

engagement_targets: Director-General's office (accountable authority, Simon Froude) — owner of Strategy 2025-2030
Assistant Director-General / division head responsible for Digital Archiving Service and collection management
Chief Information Officer / digital and ICT capability lead
Chief Financial Officer / Chief Operating Officer (financial sustainability, repository consolidation)
Chief Risk Officer / Risk Management Framework owner and the Advisory Council / audit and risk committee
Branch responsible for Agency Engagement and the 'Building trust in the public record' policy / Check-up survey
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Engagement team (First Nations description and access work)
talking_points: Your plan commits to a digital-first, end-to-end archive but admits legacy processes are 'largely manual' — we build and integrate the ingestion and workflow automation, not just advise on it.
The at-risk digitisation and format-migration targets (>=20% physical, >=95% stable digital) are measurable and recurring — we can deliver scaled digitisation and migration against them.

You state expenses 'appear likely to exceed available appropriations' — an activity-based costing and financial-sustainability review can quantify efficiencies and support repository-consolidation decisions.
You're reviewing the state/territory service model and redeveloping the Agency Engagement and Digital Archiving Service models — a target operating model and change plan can de-risk that.

Metadata, search and discovery sit at the centre of your access mandate, and you've removed the old description target while you consider 'new approaches and technology' — a metadata strategy and search uplift addresses that directly.

With the Tune Review response and Archives Act modernisation underway, independent implementation and governance support can keep a multi-year reform on track under fiscal and Strategic Commissioning constraints.
caveats: The plan is clear and rich in operational detail, giving high confidence on the digital, data, costing and operating-model opportunities; however, NAA is a smaller cultural/data agency under explicit fiscal pressure, so engagements must be right-sized (short advisories and framework/build pieces, not large embedded programs). The APS Strategic Commissioning Framework explicitly targets reduced outsourcing of Information and Knowledge Management work, which may constrain some ICT/IKM offerings toward capability transfer. There is a date discrepancy: the source URL references the 2024-25-2027-28 plan but the text is the 2025-26-2028-29 plan. External research was truncated by a tool limit: news items are headline-level only (full text not verified), and the ANAO and Parliamentary categories could not be searched — both should be re-run before client use, as the empty lists reflect incomplete searching, not confirmed absence of material.
